catalyst_summary: Invivyd (IVVD) announced positive Phase 1/2 clinical trial data for VYD2311, a monoclonal antibody for COVID-19 prevention and treatment, on February 3, 2025, showing favorable safety, pharmacokinetics, and antiviral activity in 40 subjects across multiple administration routes.[1][2]

Cue Biopharma announced positive updated Phase 1 clinical trial data for CUE-101 and CUE-102 on October 29, 2024, at SITC, including 50% ORR with CUE-101 plus pembrolizumab in low PD-L1 head and neck cancer patients and 67% DCR with CUE-102 monotherapy in late-stage pancreatic cancer (data cutoff October 29, 2024).

Taysha Gene Therapies announced preliminary phase 1/2 REVEAL trial results for TSHA-102 in Rett syndrome around July-August 2023, showing safety with no serious adverse events at 6 weeks and efficacy improvements in the first patient at 4 weeks post-dosing on scales like CGI-I, CGI-S, and RSBQ.[1][2][4]

Top-line results from the Phase I proof-of-concept clinical trial of TVGN 489 were announced by Tevogen Bio on October 8, 2024, showing the therapy was well-tolerated with rapid viral elimination, no dose-limiting toxicities, and persistence through six months in high-risk COVID-19 patients.

On October 16, 2025, Praxis Precision Medicines announced positive Phase 3 results for ulixacaltamide in essential tremor, with the drug meeting primary endpoints and all key secondary endpoints achieving statistical significance.

EyePoint Pharmaceuticals announced positive topline efficacy and safety data from the Phase 2 DAVIO 2 clinical trial of EYP-1901 in wet AMD in December 2023, achieving all primary and secondary endpoints with no drug-related serious adverse events.

Cellectis announced preliminary clinical trial results for UCART22 in r/r B-ALL and UCART20x22 in r/r B-cell NHL in their Q4 and full-year 2023 financial results report, including response rates, safety data, and patient enrollment updates as of July 1, 2023.[1]
Sagimet Biosciences announced positive topline results from the Phase 2b FASCINATE-2 clinical trial of denifanstat in biopsy-confirmed MASH patients with F2/F3 fibrosis in January 2024.[1][2]

Celcuity (CELC) announced topline results from the PIK3CA wild-type cohort of the Phase 3 VIKTORIA-1 trial on July 28, 2025, via conference call, with detailed efficacy and safety results presented at the 2025 ESMO Congress showing significant PFS improvements and good tolerability.

Sana Biotechnology announced positive preliminary 12-week clinical results for UP421, a hypoimmune-modified allogeneic pancreatic islet cell therapy for type 1 diabetes without immunosuppression, on January 7, 2025, showing stable C-peptide production, graft survival via MRI, and no safety issues.[3][1]

ProMIS Neurosciences announced on July 21, 2025, that PMN310 received FDA Fast Track Designation for Alzheimer's Disease treatment, based on encouraging Phase 1a trial results showing selective targeting of toxic amyloid-beta forms.

Unicycive announced on March 6, 2023, that the primary endpoint was met in its pivotal bioequivalence study comparing Renazorb to Fosrenol in healthy volunteers, establishing pharmacodynamic bioequivalence and satisfying FDA clinical requirements for NDA filing via the 505(b)(2) pathway.[7]

Wave Life Sciences announced interim Phase 1 clinical trial results for WVE-007 in the INLIGHT trial in December 2025, showing a single 240 mg dose achieved fat loss comparable to GLP-1 at three months with muscle preservation.

NervGen Pharma announced positive topline data from the chronic cohort of their Phase 1b/2a trial of NVG-291 for spinal cord injury, meeting the primary endpoint of statistical significance on motor evoked potential amplitude, though the exact announcement date is not specified in available sources and predates expectations for early June 2025.[1][2]

C4 Therapeutics (CCCC) shared monotherapy clinical data for cemsidomide in multiple myeloma in December 2023, demonstrating immune cell activation at clinically relevant doses, around the time of their oncology deal announcement on 2023-12-13.[7][8][9]

FDA approved Eli Lilly's Omvoh (mirikizumab) for Crohn's disease on October 14, 2024, based on positive Phase III VIVID-1 trial results showing 53% clinical remission versus 36% on placebo at one year.[3]

Shattuck Labs announced positive initial topline data from a Phase 1A/B dose expansion trial of SL-172154 in combination with azacitidine on December 13, 2023, showing a 79% objective response rate in frontline HR-MDS patients and 27% initial complete response in frontline TP53m AML patients.

PepGen announced clinical trial results from the 15 mg/kg cohort of the FREEDOM-DM1 Phase 1 trial, reporting a mean splicing correction of 53.7% in DM1 patients around late 2025, including November 13, 2025, but no specific announcement on or around September 25, 2025.

On February 27, 2024, Viking Therapeutics announced positive Phase 2 VENTURE trial results showing VK2735 achieved its primary endpoint with statistically significant body weight reductions up to 14.7% compared to placebo, with up to 88% of patients achieving at least 10% weight loss.

ProMIS Neurosciences announced positive Phase 1a clinical trial results for PMN310 on July 17, 2023, showing the drug was well tolerated and demonstrated central nervous system exposure indicating target engagement in Alzheimer's disease.[3]

Invivyd announced interim exploratory clinical data from the ongoing Phase 3 CANOPY trial for VYD222 on or around December 2023, showing a potential early signal of clinical protection from symptomatic COVID-19 with 0% infection rate in the VYD222 arm versus 3% in placebo as of December 1, 2023 (median 67 days follow-up).[2]

On September 8, 2025, Rapport Therapeutics announced positive Phase 2a topline results for RAP-219 in drug-resistant focal onset seizures, showing 77.8% median seizure reduction and 24% seizure freedom.

Cogent Biosciences (COGT) announced positive top-line Phase 3 PEAK trial results on November 10, 2025, showing bezuclastinib plus sunitinib reduced progression or death risk by 50% (HR 0.50) versus sunitinib alone in imatinib-resistant/intolerant GIST patients, with median PFS of 16.5 vs. 9.2 months and ORR of 46% vs. 26%.

Phase III trial results for GMRx2, a novel triple combination pill (telmisartan, amlodipine, and indapamide) for hypertension, were published in The Lancet on October 18, 2024, showing superior blood pressure reduction and control rates compared to dual therapies with good tolerability.[1][4]
Tectonic Therapeutic (TECX) announced positive interim data from the Phase 1b trial of TX45 in patients with Group 2 Pulmonary Hypertension in HFpEF on January 30, 2025.

Corcept Therapeutics (CORT) announced on March 31, 2025, that their Phase 3 ROSELLA trial of relacorilant plus nab-paclitaxel met its primary endpoint of improved progression-free survival (6.5 vs. 5.5 months, HR 0.70, p=0.008) in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer patients, with positive interim overall survival data (16.0 vs. 11.5 months, HR 0.69, p=0.012).[1][2]

On August 7, 2023, Tango Therapeutics announced proof-of-mechanism data for TNG908 showing marked SDMA reduction in MTAP-deleted cancer cells, with 16 patients treated across dose escalation cohorts as of May 9, 2023.

Armata Pharmaceuticals presented late-breaking positive results from its Phase 2a diSArm study of AP-SA02, a Staphylococcus aureus bacteriophage cocktail, at IDWeek 2025 in October 2025, showing 88% clinical response rate at Day 12 versus 58% in placebo and zero non-response/relapse rates in the AP-SA02 group.

Inhibrx announced positive topline results from the ChonDRAgon registrational trial on October 23, 2025, showing ozekibart achieved a 52% reduction in disease progression or death risk with median progression-free survival of 5.52 months versus 2.66 months for placebo in advanced chondrosarcoma.

MBX Biosciences announced positive Phase 2 topline results for canvuparatide in hypoparathyroidism on September 22, 2025, showing a 63% responder rate at 12 weeks versus 31% placebo and 79% at six months in the open-label extension.

SAB Biotherapeutics announced positive Phase 3 trial results for SAB-185 on April 26, 2023, showing benefits in sustained symptom resolution for COVID-19 patients compared to REGEN-COV, though the primary endpoint was inconclusive due to low event rates.[1]
LP-10 phase 2a clinical trial top-line results were reported in January 2023.

Biomea Fusion announced initial positive topline data from the first two cohorts of patients with type 2 diabetes on March 28, 2023, showing that BMF-219, a menin inhibitor, demonstrated HbA1c reductions and improved beta cell function.

Preliminary clinical trial results were published on Spruce Biosciences's website in August 2025 showing that TA-ERT reduced heparan sulfate to normal or near-normal levels over a five-year span and appeared to stop cognitive decline in patients with Sanfilippo Syndrome Type B (MPS IIIB).

C4 Therapeutics announced positive Phase 1 clinical trial data for CFT7455 in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma on December 12, 2023, showing it was well tolerated with evidence of anti-myeloma activity and immunomodulatory effects in heavily pretreated patients.[1][4]

On September 26, 2023, Immunovant announced positive Phase 1 clinical trial results for IMVT-1402, showing that subcutaneous doses achieved IgG reductions similar to batoclimab with favorable safety profiles and no adverse effects on serum albumin or LDL cholesterol.

Innate Pharma announced updated Phase 1/2 clinical trial results for SAR443579/IPH6101 on or around December 13, 2024, showing clinical benefit with 5 complete remissions (4 CR/1 CRi) at 1 mg/kg and durable responses in R/R AML patients.[1]

Korro Bio announced disappointing Phase 1/2a REWRITE trial results for KRRO-110 in alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, showing the drug failed to reach projected protein levels in patients, prompting a strategic pivot to GalNAc delivery.

On or around August 7, 2023, Nektar announced corrected Phase 2b REZOLVE-AD clinical trial data for rezpegaldesleukin (rezpeg) in atopic dermatitis, showing improved efficacy metrics such as 36% placebo-adjusted EASI improvement for the high dose versus Lilly's prior 17% analysis.

Tectonic Therapeutic (TECX) announced favorable Phase 1a topline data for TX45 on or around June 21, 2024, demonstrating safety, tolerability, and favorable PK/PD profile in healthy volunteers, with no observed immunogenicity.[1]

No clinical trial data or results for ARTV (Artiva Biotherapeutics' AlloNK) were announced on or around December 15, 2025; the closest announcements were preliminary safety and translational data from 32 patients reported on November 12, 2025.

MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (MLTX) announced mixed Phase 3 VELA trial results for sonelokimab in hidradenitis suppurativa on September 28-29, 2025, where VELA-1 met its primary endpoint (HiSCR75 35% vs 18% placebo) but VELA-2 missed statistical significance (p=0.053) due to high placebo response, though significant under treatment policy strategy.

Avalo Therapeutics announced topline results from the Phase 2 PEAK trial of AVTX-002 (quisovalimab) in non-eosinophilic asthma on June 26, 2023, reporting that the trial failed to meet its primary endpoint of reducing asthma-related events, though the drug demonstrated a favorable safety profile and showed a trend toward fewer asthma events in patients with high baseline LIGHT levels.

Microbot Medical (MBOT) announced on January 3, 2025, the successful completion of its ACCESS-PVI pivotal human clinical trial for the LIBERTY Endovascular Robotic System, with full results detailed later on April 9, 2025, showing 100% robotic navigation success and 92% radiation reduction.[4][1]

Mereo BioPharma (MREO) announced on December 29, 2025, that the Phase 3 ORBIT and COSMIC studies for setrusumab in Osteogenesis Imperfecta showed significant BMD improvements but failed to meet primary endpoints on fracture rates.[1][2][5]

Aclaris Therapeutics (ACRS) announced top-line results on November 13, 2023, for its Phase 2b clinical trial of zunsemetinib in rheumatoid arthritis, which failed to meet the primary endpoint of ACR20 response or any secondary endpoints at both 20mg and 50mg doses.
Kazia Therapeutics announced Phase II/III trial results for paxalisib in glioblastoma on July 10, 2024, showing a 3.8-month improvement in overall survival for newly diagnosed unmethylated patients.

No clinical trial data or results for PALI-2108 were announced on or around January 29, 2024; available announcements report positive preliminary Phase 1a/b data later in 2024 and 2025, including safety, tolerability, and efficacy in ulcerative colitis patients.[1][2][3]

Spruce Biosciences (SPRB) announced integrated long-term clinical data from studies 201, 202, 401, and 902 for tralesinidase alfa (TA-ERT) in Sanfilippo Syndrome Type B (MPS IIIB), showing rapid reductions in disease biomarker heparan sulfate, stabilization of cognition, communication, motor skills, and cortical grey matter volume compared to declines in untreated patients.[1][2]

Cytokinetics announced positive topline results from the SEQUOIA-HCM Phase 3 clinical trial of aficamten in patients with obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy on December 27, 2023, showing statistically significant improvements in exercise capacity and all secondary endpoints.

Relmada Therapeutics presented positive Phase 2 clinical trial data for NDV-01 on April 28, 2025, showing an 85% overall response rate at 3 months and 90% of patients achieving high-grade disease-free status in non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.

On March 8, 2024, Amylyx Pharmaceuticals announced topline results from the Phase 3 PHOENIX trial showing that AMX0035 failed to meet its primary and secondary endpoints in treating ALS, with no statistically significant difference compared to placebo.
Biosyngen presented next-generation TIL technology data at the ESMO 2024 Annual Meeting in September 2024, with BRG01 in Phase II trials for solid tumors and BST02 and BRL03 in Phase I trials.

Elicio Therapeutics (ELTX) announced positive interim Phase 1 data for ELI-002 2P on June 2-6, 2023, showing it was well-tolerated with no dose-limiting toxicities, 77% tumor biomarker reduction, 32% clearance, and robust mKRAS-specific T cell responses in 87% of patients with high-relapse-risk pancreatic and colorectal cancers.[1]

Neumora Therapeutics (NMRA) announced topline results from the Phase 3 KOASTAL-1 study of navacaprant in major depressive disorder on January 2, 2025, which failed to meet the primary endpoint of MADRS total score reduction and key secondary endpoint compared to placebo, though showing an efficacy signal in female participants.

AVTX-002 (quisovalimab) Phase 2 PEAK trial in non-eosinophilic asthma did not meet its primary endpoint for reduction in asthma-related events, though positive trends were observed in a patient subpopulation with elevated baseline LIGHT levels.

Ventyx Biosciences announced Phase 2 SERENITY trial results for VTX958 in moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis on November 6-7, 2023, meeting primary and key secondary endpoints but with efficacy below internal targets, leading to termination of development in psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis.[1][2][3][5]
On October 31, 2023, TuHURA Biosciences announced preliminary topline results for VAL-083 from the GBM AGILE study, which did not perform better than the standard of care.[8]

Korro Bio announced clinical trial results for KRRO-110 in AATD patients on November 12, 2025, showing functional M-AAT protein production but below projected levels (peak ~2-3 µM increase vs. 11 µM threshold) as of a November 6 data cutoff, leading to stock drop and strategic pivot.[1][2][5]

Clinical trial results for ADI-PEG 20 (pegargiminase, also known as ATOMIC-Meso) in mesothelioma were published in 2024, showing improved median overall survival of 9.3 months versus 7.7 months with chemotherapy alone, though no specific announcement on or around March 26, 2024, is detailed in available sources.

No clinical trial data or results for LONA were announced on or around 2024-09-04; the closest match is Longeveron's Phase 2b trial results for laromestrocel (Lomecel-B) in age-related frailty, published October 29, 2024, showing a 63.4-meter improvement in 6-minute walk test at month 9 (p=0.0077).

No clinical trial data or results for SPRY (Spry Belt device for osteopenia) were announced on or around 2023-05-12; the referenced UNMC article from January 2023 discusses an ongoing study without specific results on that date.[4][6]

MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (MLTX) announced positive topline 12-week results from the Phase 2 MIRA trial of sonelokimab in hidradenitis suppurativa on June 25, 2023, meeting the primary HiSCR75 endpoint with 43.3% and 34.8% response rates for 120mg and 240mg doses versus 14.7% placebo.[1][4]

On December 4, 2024, Relmada Therapeutics (RLMD) announced that the Data Monitoring Committee determined the Phase 3 Reliance II trial of REL-1017 was futile and unlikely to meet its primary efficacy endpoint, with no new safety concerns identified.

BioAge Labs announced on December 6, 2024, the discontinuation of its STRIDES Phase 2 clinical trial for azelaprag in combination with tirzepatide due to unexpected liver transaminitis observed in some patients, with no full efficacy results released.

Cumberland Pharmaceuticals (CPIX) announced positive top-line results from the Phase 2 FIGHT DMD trial of ifetroban on February 4, 2025, showing significant improvements in left ventricular ejection fraction and reductions in cardiac damage markers in DMD patients.

On January 12, 2023, Oramed announced top-line results from the Phase 3 ORA-D-013-1 trial of ORMD-0801 oral insulin for type 2 diabetes, which failed to meet its primary endpoint of A1C reduction and secondary endpoint of fasting plasma glucose at 26 weeks, with no serious drug-related adverse events reported.

Annovis Bio (ANVS) announced positive Phase III Parkinson's disease study results for buntanetap on July 2, 2024, showing statistically significant improvements in MDS-UPDRS scores, motor/non-motor functions, and cognition, particularly in patients with longer disease duration and PIGD.

On June 17, 2024, Ovid Therapeutics announced Takeda's Phase 3 topline results for soticlestat, which narrowly missed the primary endpoint of reduction in convulsive seizure frequency in Dravet syndrome (SKYLINE trial, p=0.06) but showed significant secondary endpoints and a favorable safety profile.

Q32 Bio (QTTB) announced Phase 2a clinical trial results for bempikibart in alopecia areata (SIGNAL-AA) and atopic dermatitis (SIGNAL-AD) on or around December 11, 2024, showing mixed outcomes with failure to meet primary endpoints, high placebo responses, and protocol issues leading to sharp stock declines.

Armata Pharmaceuticals (ARMP) announced positive topline results from the Phase 1b/2a diSArm trial of AP-SA02 on May 19, 2025, showing 88% clinical response rate at day 12 versus 58% for placebo, with 100% response at later points and no serious adverse events.[2][3][4][7]

Q32 Bio announced completion of enrollment in Part B of the SIGNAL-AA Phase 2a trial on November 13, 2025, with 33 patients enrolled and emerging signs of clinical activity observed in early-enrolling patients.

Wave Life Sciences announced positive proof-of-mechanism clinical trial data from the RestorAATion-2 study of WVE-006 on October 16, 2024, demonstrating the first-ever therapeutic RNA editing in humans with wild-type M-AAT protein reaching over 60% of total AAT in the first two patients after a single dose.

No clinical trial data or results for ENGN were announced on or around October 24, 2023; Phase 1 LEGEND study results were reported in enGene's full-year 2023 financial update (likely March 2024) and presented at the Society of Urologic Oncology meeting in November 2023, with ENGN's public launch announced on October 24, 2023.

On November 15, 2023, MoonLake Immunotherapeutics announced positive top-line results from the Phase 2 ARGO trial evaluating sonelokimab in patients with psoriatic arthritis, which met its primary endpoint with statistically significant ACR50 response compared to placebo at week 12.
Galecto announced on August 15, 2023, that its Phase 2b GALACTIC-1 trial of GB0139 for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) failed to meet its primary endpoint, resulting in a 71% stock price decline.

Ventyx Biosciences announced positive topline results from a Phase 2 study of VTX3232 on October 22, 2025, showing significant reductions in hsCRP (64-78%) and other inflammation markers in participants with obesity and cardiovascular risk factors, meeting primary safety endpoints.[1][2][3]

Protara Therapeutics announced positive Phase 2 ADVANCED-2 trial results for TARA-002 in NMIBC patients on December 5, 2024, with data cutoff on November 19, 2024, showing high complete response rates in BCG-unresponsive and BCG-naïve cohorts and a favorable safety profile.[1][6]

GH Research announced on February 3, 2025, that the Phase 2b trial of GH001 in treatment-resistant depression met its primary endpoint with a significant placebo-adjusted MADRS reduction of -15.5 points on Day 8 (p<0.0001), along with all secondary endpoints met and good tolerability.

Viking Therapeutics announced Phase 1 clinical trial results on March 28, 2023, showing VK2735 demonstrated up to 7.8% mean body weight loss from baseline and 6.0% placebo-adjusted weight loss over 28 days with safe and well-tolerated safety profile.

Lyell Immunopharma announced promising Phase 1 trial results for LYL273 on or around June 4, 2025, showing a 67% overall response rate and 83% disease control rate in refractory metastatic colorectal cancer patients at the highest dose level.
Palisade Bio announced Phase 1b clinical trial results for PALI-2108 on or around December 2024, reporting 100% clinical response in ulcerative colitis patients with no serious adverse events.
No clinical trial data or results were announced for OM on or around 2024-08-08 based on the available search results.

On December 11, 2025, a CBS News video announced the launch of the MEOW-1 clinical trial by Okava Pharmaceuticals testing the GLP-1 implant OKV-119 for weight loss in obese cats, but no trial results or data were announced.[7]

Candel Therapeutics (CADL) announced on December 11, 2024, that its Phase 3 trial of CAN-2409 in intermediate-to-high-risk localized prostate cancer met its primary endpoint of statistically significant improvement in disease-free survival.[1][3]

PepGen announced initial positive clinical trial data from the 5 mg/kg and 10 mg/kg cohorts of the ongoing FREEDOM-DM1 Phase 1 study for PGN-EDODM1 in myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) on February 24, 2025, showing mean splicing corrections of 12.3% and 29.1%, respectively.[5][6]

On October 16, 2023, Aldeyra Therapeutics (ALDX) announced minutes from a late-cycle FDA review meeting for the reproxalap NDA in dry eye disease, indicating the potential need for an additional clinical trial rather than new trial results or data.[4][5][6][7]

Praxis Precision Medicines announced topline results from the Essential1 Phase 3 study of ulixacaltamide for essential tremor on March 3, 2023, showing numerical improvement in the primary endpoint.[3][5]

MoonLake Immunotherapeutics referenced positive MIRA trial data showing a 43% HiSCR75 response rate with 120mg sonelokimab (29 ppt delta vs placebo, p<0.001) at week 12 in their FDA Type B meeting announcement, but no new results were announced specifically on or around 2024-03-08; a presentation was planned for the AAD 2024 meeting.[1][4]

Incannex (IXHL) announced positive topline results from the RePOSA Phase 2 clinical trial of IHL-42X for obstructive sleep apnea on or around July 18, 2025, showing up to 83% AHI reduction, significant improvements in sleep quality and patient outcomes, and an excellent safety profile with no serious adverse events.
Immutep (IMMP) announced positive clinical trial data from Part A of the Phase II TACTI-002 study in first-line NSCLC on May 17, 2023, causing shares to surge 64.8%.[6]

Vera Therapeutics announced positive topline results from the Phase 2b ORIGIN clinical trial on January 3-4, 2023, showing that atacicept met its primary endpoint with a 33% mean reduction in proteinuria in the 150 mg dose group at 24 weeks for IgA nephropathy treatment.

Kala Pharmaceuticals announced positive safety data from Cohort 1 of the CHASE Phase 2b clinical trial for KPI-012 on March 27, 2023, showing no safety issues in two patients treated with the high dose, with topline efficacy data targeted for Q1 2024.[2]

Cardiff Oncology (CRDF) announced a clinical update on February 29, 2024, from its Phase 2 randomized second-line ONSEMBLE trial in RAS-mutated mCRC, providing new data on onvansertib's efficacy in bev-naïve patients, though no results from the first-line CRDF-004 trial were released on or around March 1, 2024 (CRDF-004 topline expected mid-2024).[2]

enGene announced on or around December 2023 that the Phase 1 portion of the LEGEND study for EG-70 in BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer achieved primary and secondary endpoints, including a 68% 3-month complete response rate in 22 patients, with the pivotal Phase 2 portion underway and interim data expected mid-2024.[1][8]

Crinetics announced on September 10, 2023, that paltusotine achieved positive results by meeting the primary endpoint and all secondary endpoints of the Phase 3 PATHFNDR-1 study in acromegaly patients.

ADC Therapeutics announced initial Phase 2 clinical trial results for ZYNLONTA in combination with rituximab on December 12, 2023, showing a 96% overall response rate and 85% complete response rate in relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma patients.

ADC Therapeutics announced on December 6, 2024, an upcoming initial data update from the LOTIS-7 Phase 1b trial evaluating ZYNLONTA in combination with glofitamab, with positive results (94% ORR, 72% CR rate) released on December 11, 2024.
No clinical trial data or results for PALI-2108 (PALI) were announced on or around August 9, 2023; that date featured topline data from the unrelated LB1148 Phase 2 PROFILE study.[8]

Verrica Pharmaceuticals announced positive top-line results from their partner Torii Pharmaceutical's confirmatory Phase 3 trial of TO-208 (VP-102) for molluscum contagiosum in Japan on December 15, 2023, showing statistically significant complete clearance versus placebo with good tolerability.[6]

InflaRx announced FDA Emergency Use Authorization for vilobelimab (Gohibic) on April 4, 2023, based on positive Phase III PANAMO trial results showing a 23.9% relative reduction in 28-day all-cause mortality versus placebo in critically ill COVID-19 patients, with a conference call scheduled for April 5, 2023.

Incannex Healthcare (IXHL) announced positive Phase 2 trial results for IHL-42X in treating obstructive sleep apnea on or around May 15, 2025, showing up to 83% AHI reduction, significant patient-reported improvements, and an excellent safety profile.[1][2][5]

Incannex (IXHL) announced positive topline results from the RePOSA Phase 2 trial of IHL-42X for obstructive sleep apnea on July 30, 2025, showing significant AHI reductions up to 83% in the high-dose group, improvements in oxygenation and patient-reported outcomes, and a strong safety profile with no serious adverse events.[1]

No clinical trial data or results for KZIA were announced on or around 2024-10-17; the relevant announcements occurred in October 2024 for Phase I data with radiation therapy and later in 2025 for TNBC responses including an initial iCR and preliminary Phase 1b partial responses.

Pliant Therapeutics (PLRX) announced a voluntary pause of dosing in the Phase 2b/3 BEACON-IPF trial for bexotegrast on or around February 10, 2025, following a data safety monitoring board review, with no new patient enrollment and the trial remaining blinded.[3][4]

On March 3, 2025, Pliant Therapeutics (PLRX) announced the discontinuation of the BEACON-IPF Phase 2b/3 trial for bexotegrast in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis due to an imbalance in IPF-related adverse events, while noting early evidence of efficacy on the forced vital capacity endpoint.
On May 28, 2025, InflaRx announced that the Independent Data Monitoring Committee recommended stopping the Phase 3 vilobelimab trial in pyoderma gangrenosum due to futility.

Annovis Bio (ANVS) announced statistically significant Phase II/III clinical trial data for buntanetap in early Alzheimer's disease on April 29, 2024, showing dose-dependent cognitive improvements in mild AD patients and reduced plasma tTau levels, though primary endpoints across all patients did not meet significance.[1][2]

ANX007 failed to meet its primary endpoint in the ARCHER Phase 2 trial for geographic atrophy, showing no statistically significant improvement in lesion area reduction compared to sham control at 12 months, though it did show a 59% reduction in risk of vision loss.

OS Therapies announced positive Phase 1 safety data for OST-HER2 in HER2-expressing solid tumors, including breast cancer patients, on August 1, 2024, confirming it was safe and well-tolerated at the dose used in the ongoing Phase 2b trial.[1][9]

enGene's 2023 Annual Report details Phase 1 interim results for EG-70 (detalimogene voraplasmid) in BCG-unresponsive NMIBC, reporting a 68% 3-month complete response rate across doses and 70% at the recommended Phase 2 dose, with data collected up to October 31, 2023.

On or around August 4, 2023, the FDA issued a Complete Response Letter (CRL) to Mesoblast's BLA resubmission for remestemcel-L in pediatric steroid-refractory acute graft-versus-host disease, referencing phase 3 trial data including survival outcomes.[5][8]

Agenus announced topline interim Phase 2 data for BOT/BAL in MSS colorectal cancer on July 18, 2024, showing an ORR of 19.4% for the BOT 75mg/BAL combination and a 6-month survival rate of 90%, consistent with Phase 1 results.[1]

NervGen Pharma announced positive topline data from the chronic cohort of its Phase 1b/2a clinical trial of NVG-291 in spinal cord injury, meeting one co-primary endpoint for improved motor connectivity, though the exact announcement date is not specified as on or around February 27, 2024, with prior updates indicating data readout expected in Q3 2024.[1][4]

DBV Technologies (DBVT) announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 VITESSE trial of the VIASKIN Peanut patch on December 16, 2025, showing 46.6% responder rate vs 14.8% placebo, with BLA submission planned for H1 2026.

Lexicon Pharmaceuticals (LXRX) scheduled the announcement of topline results from the Phase 2b PROGRESS study of pilavapadin for diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain on March 3, 2025, with subsequent data presentations confirming a two-point reduction in average daily pain scores by week 12 at the 10 mg dose.[1][2]
EyePoint Pharmaceuticals announced positive interim 16-week data from the ongoing Phase 2 VERONA clinical trial of DURAVYU for Diabetic Macular Edema on April 21, 2023.[6]

No clinical trial data or results for NTHI (NeOnc Technologies) were announced on or around 2025-07-02; the nearest relevant announcement was a strategic term sheet on July 08, 2025, with updated clinical results from Phase 1/2a trials for intranasal NEO100 reported later on December 15, 2025.

On November 2, 2023, Dogwood Therapeutics received a Nasdaq listing notification letter, and around that time the company announced results from the Bateman Horne Center's investigator-initiated study showing that IMC-2 (valacyclovir and celecoxib combination) demonstrated statistically significant reductions in Long-COVID related fatigue, orthostatic intolerance, pain, and anxiety.

Alterity Therapeutics (ATHE) announced positive topline results from the ATH434-201 Phase 2 clinical trial in early-stage multiple system atrophy (MSA) patients on January 30, 2025, showing statistically significant slowing of clinical progression with the 50 mg dose (48% at 52 weeks, p=0.03) and good tolerability.[3][4]

Ovid Therapeutics announced Phase 1 clinical trial results for OV350, an IV KCC2 activator, on August 13, 2025, as part of their Q2 2025 financial results, showing it met safety, tolerability, and PK objectives with central activity via qEEG, though the IV program will not advance further in favor of oral candidates like OV4071.[1][3]
ALX Oncology (ALXO) announced positive interim Phase 2 ASPEN-06 clinical trial results for evorpacept on October 3, 2023, showing a confirmed ORR of 52% versus 22% for control.

On February 24, 2023, Fulcrum announced Phase 1b trial data for FTX-6058 showing HbF increases up to 9.5% in sickle cell disease patients, but the FDA simultaneously placed a clinical hold on the trial due to preclinical safety concerns related to potential hematological malignancies associated with PRC2 inhibitors.

Tonix Pharmaceuticals announced positive topline results from a Phase 1 trial of TNX-1500 on February 6, 2025, showing the drug blocked primary and secondary antibody responses at 10 mg/kg and 30 mg/kg doses with a mean half-life of 34-38 days supporting monthly dosing.

Cardiff Oncology announced positive initial Phase 2 CRDF-004 trial data on December 10, 2024, showing 64% objective response rate with the 30mg dose of onvansertib plus standard care versus 33% with standard care alone in first-line RAS-mutated metastatic colorectal cancer.

Maze Therapeutics announced positive Phase 1 clinical trial results for MZE782, an SLC6A19 inhibitor for PKU and CKD, on September 11-12, 2025, showing dose-dependent increases in urinary phenylalanine excretion up to 42-fold, a clean safety profile, and reversible eGFR dips in 112 healthy volunteers.

Acumen Pharmaceuticals announced positive Phase 1 INTERCEPT-AD trial results for ACU193, an amyloid-beta oligomer targeting antibody therapy for early Alzheimer's disease, demonstrating a favorable safety profile and dose-related central target engagement.

Edgewise Therapeutics (EWTX) announced positive top-line data from a Phase 1 trial in healthy subjects and Phase 2 CIRRUS-HCM Part A trial in obstructive HCM patients on September 19, 2024, showing significant reductions in LVOT gradients without meaningful LVEF changes.

No search results indicate any clinical trial data or results for TIL therapies were announced on or around September 9, 2024; the closest references are to a November 20, 2024 data cutoff for lifileucel and earlier approvals or interim data without specific mention of that date.

No clinical trial data or results for TVGN were announced on or around October 23, 2024; the Phase I proof-of-concept topline results were announced on October 8, 2024, with related press releases on October 25, 2024 referencing prior data.

Enlivex announced positive Phase II trial results on April 11, 2024, showing 65% reduction in overall mortality and substantial SOFA score reductions in sepsis patients, with a favorable safety profile.

Equilibre Biopharmaceuticals announced positive Phase 2 topline results for EQU-001 showing a 35.7% median reduction in focal seizure frequency over placebo in the 60 mg treatment arm, with a clean safety profile.

Tenaya Therapeutics (TNYA) announced late-breaker presentation of new interim clinical trial data from the MyPEAK-1 Phase 1b/2 trial of TN-201 on March 31, 2025, at the ACC Annual Meeting, showing TN-201 was well-tolerated with robust transduction, increasing RNA/protein expression, and clinical improvements in Cohort 1 patients.[1][3][4]

Wave Life Sciences announced data from Part A of the WVE-N531 trial in DMD, demonstrating industry-leading exon skipping levels of 53% and muscle tissue results, as part of their Q4 and full-year 2023 financial results report.[1]

Contineum Therapeutics (CTNM) provided an update on its Phase 1b PET trial of PIPE-791 on June 25, 2025, but stock fell due to a delay in trial data release, with no full results announced on or around June 20, 2025.
Ferring Pharmaceuticals announced on December 3, 2024, that it advanced three clinical studies in its ADSTILADRIN program for non-muscle invasive bladder cancer and upper tract urothelial cancer.

NovaBridge Biosciences (NBP) announced positive Phase 1b clinical trial results for Givastomig on or around December 2-4, 2025, showing 71-77% ORR and 16.9 months mPFS in gastroesophageal cancer patients, not on August 1, 2025.

Biomea Fusion announced Phase I COVALENT-101 trial results for BMF-219 on December 11, 2023, showing achievement of minimal residual disease negativity in the first complete responder in AML patients, with early clinical activity, good tolerability, and no dose-limiting toxicities.[1]
